# Local Cache Core

HydraCache must be useful before any database adapter or distributed feature exists.

The Phase 0 promise is intentionally small:

- create a cache
- get a value
- put a value
- load on miss
- attach TTL and tags
- invalidate by key or tag

```rust
let cache = HydraCache::local()
    .default_ttl(Duration::from_secs(300))
    .max_capacity(100_000)
    .build()?;

let profile = cache
    .get_or_load(
        "profile:user:42",
        CacheOptions::new()
            .ttl(Duration::from_secs(300))
            .tags(["user:42", "users_table"]),
        || async { load_profile(user_id).await },
    )
    .await?;
```

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-local-cache-flow
%%| fig-cap: "The local cache-aside flow."
flowchart LR
  A[Caller] --> B[HydraCache get_or_load]
  B --> C{Cache hit?}
  C -->|yes| D[Decode and return]
  C -->|no| E[Run loader]
  E --> F[Store value with TTL and tags]
  F --> D
```

## Source Notes

Use these files when expanding this chapter:

- [Local cache core note](../learning/local-cache-core.md)
- [Moka reread](../../../moka/MOKA_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [Caffeine reread](../../../caffeine/CAFFEINE_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [HikariCP reread](../../../hikaricp/HIKARICP_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
